$note: Phase-3 misconception tag-lists, produced by MisconceptionAnalyst from the harvested_from provenance in the misconception_suggestions_*.json files. For each topic, each bucket's verbatim common_miss is paired with the canonical misconception id(s) an author should attach to the matching must_not_include entry (object form {phrase, misconception}). Where an entry lists more than one id, all apply (a must_not_include entry may carry an array of ids per thread v5). Registry basis: 17 families / 67 misconceptions (thread v5). See inter_chat/Architecture_MisconceptionAnalyst_registry_harvest.md.
present_indicative_note: INCLUDED as topics['verb_form.present_indicative'] (added 2026-07-12, thread v8): 18 buckets (16 formation matched to v1 seeds + 2 usage mapped to the discrimination axis). Note: stem_change.isc_under_application is a v1 seed with no 1:1 present bucket.

adjective_agreement.common_miss: case grande (no -i marking for Class II plural)

adjective_agreement.note: transfer is a valid secondary co-tag (English fronting).

adjective_agreement.common_miss: verdi scure (full agreement) or verde scure (half agreement)

pronoun.common_miss: Marco vede me (using stressed pronoun instead)
gli (confusion with IOP) or li' (incorrect elision)
gli lo dico (not attached) or le lo dico (no glie- transform)

pronoun.note: transfer secondary co-tag for the post-finite-verb (English order) case.

pronoun.common_miss: Voglio lo vedere (separated) or vedere lo (separated)
pronoun.note: transfer secondary co-tag for the post-finite-verb (English order) case.
transfer secondary co-tag for the post-finite-verb (English order) case.
transfer secondary co-tag for the post-finite-verb (English order) case.

pronoun.common_miss: Mi dì! / Lo dammi! (separated or wrong)
pronoun.note: transfer secondary co-tag for the post-finite-verb (English order) case.

pronoun.common_miss: dami / dimmilo / dami lo (single consonant or wrong cluster)

pronoun.note: transfer secondary co-tag for the post-finite-verb (English order) case.

pronoun.common_miss: Le ha piaciuto i fiori (using avere) or Le sono piaciuto (no agreement with grammatical subject)
Non lo faccio (omitting the ci+la cluster)

pronoun.note: INTENTIONAL NON-MAPPING (common_miss carries no taggable error pattern)

tense_choice.common_miss: over-applying the progressive for any English -ing form
over-applying the past progressive for any English 'was -ing'
using present for cases where future is required (distant, uncertain, or probabilistic)
over-applying the morphological future when a present-for-future is more natural
using present indicative + future ('se ho tempo, verrò') which is fine for a real condition but wrong for a counterfactual
treating the colloquial form as wrong when in fact it's accepted in speech
using indicative ('penso che è'), accepted in speech but not in writing
using indicative ('sei qui'), more clearly wrong than after opinion verbs
using indicative after these conjunctions, much rarer in speech than after opinion verbs
keeping indicative under negation when subjunctive is now triggered
using imperfetto for a clearly-completed prior event ('mangiava' for 'had eaten')
using trapassato for an ongoing state ('avevo mangiato quando sei entrato' implies I'd finished, which usually isn't the intended reading)

verb_form.condizionale.common_miss: single-m 1pl (parleremo, which is the future) instead of the double-m conditional (parleremmo); or keeping the -are a (parlarei)
verb_form.condizionale.note: co-tag gemination.geminate_simplified at item level (the -emmo/-emo surface is a geminate contrast); paradigm_confusion is primary.

verb_form.condizionale.common_miss: single-m 1pl (prenderemo, the future) for the double-m conditional (prenderemmo)
verb_form.condizionale.note: co-tag gemination.geminate_simplified at item level (the -emmo/-emo surface is a geminate contrast); paradigm_confusion is primary.
co-tag gemination.geminate_simplified at item level (the -emmo/-emo surface is a geminate contrast); paradigm_confusion is primary.
co-tag gemination.geminate_simplified at item level (the -emmo/-emo surface is a geminate contrast); paradigm_confusion is primary.

verb_form.condizionale.common_miss: keeping the redundant i (comincierei), or single-m 1pl
verb_form.condizionale.note: co-tag gemination.geminate_simplified at item level (the -emmo/-emo surface is a geminate contrast); paradigm_confusion is primary.

verb_form.condizionale.common_miss: keeping the theme vowel (averei, poterei), or single-m 1pl (avremo, the future)
verb_form.condizionale.note: co-tag gemination.geminate_simplified at item level (the -emmo/-emo surface is a geminate contrast); paradigm_confusion is primary.

verb_form.condizionale.common_miss: single r (vorei, verei) instead of the assimilated -rr-
building from the infinitive (esserei), or single-m 1pl (saremo, the future)
verb_form.condizionale.note: co-tag gemination.geminate_simplified at item level (the -emmo/-emo surface is a geminate contrast); paradigm_confusion is primary.

verb_form.condizionale.common_miss: applying the a-to-e shift (derei, ferei) instead of keeping -ar-
wrong auxiliary, or participle not agreeing with essere (sarei andato for a feminine subject who needs andata)

verb_form.congiuntivo.common_miss: using the indicative 3pl -ano (parlano) instead of the subjunctive -ino (parlino); or an indicative singular (parla)
using -i (the -are subjunctive ending) on an -ere/-ire verb (prendi); or the indicative 3pl -ono (prendono) for -ano
carrying -isc- into the 1pl/2pl (capiscamo) or omitting it in the singular (capa)
omitting the h (ceri) or keeping the redundant i (comincii)
building from the infinitive instead of the 1sg present (venga written as vena); wrong 3pl (vengono, the indicative, for vengano)
deriving from the 1sg present (sono gives wrong sona instead of sia; ho gives wrong abba instead of abbia)
single s (parlasi) for the double-s (parlassi); wrong class vowel (parlessi)
regularising the stem (facessi written as farei-style faressi; dessi as dassi)
using the indicative auxiliary (ha parlato) instead of the subjunctive (abbia parlato); participle not agreeing with sia
using the imperfect indicative auxiliary (aveva parlato) instead of the imperfect subjunctive (avessi parlato); participle not agreeing with fosse

verb_form.future.common_miss: keeping the -are a (parlarò) instead of shifting to e, or dropping the accent on parlerò / parlerà
dropping the accent on the 1sg/3sg, or syncopating a regular -ere verb by false analogy (scrivrò for scriverò)
carrying -isc- into the future (capiscirò for capirò), or dropping the 1sg/3sg accent
omitting the h (cercerò, pageremo), which softens the consonant
keeping the theme vowel (avero, andero, potero) instead of syncopating to avr-, andr-, potr-
writing a single r (vorò, verò, terò) instead of the assimilated -rr-
building from the infinitive (esserò, essrò) instead of the sar- stem, or dropping the accent on sarà
applying the a-to-e shift (derò, ferò, sterò) instead of keeping -ar-
wrong auxiliary, or participle not agreeing with essere (sarò andato for a feminine subject who needs andata)

verb_form.gerundio.common_miss: carrying -isc- (capiscendo), writing -iendo (capiendo), or using -ando on an -ere/-ire verb (prendando)
building from the contracted infinitive (facendo written as farendo, bevendo as berendo)
wrong stare form for the person (sto / stai confusion), or pairing with an infinitive instead of a gerund (sto parlare)
verb_form.gerundio.note: the stare-person half of this bucket also tags agreement.auxiliary_person.

verb_form.gerundio.common_miss: wrong auxiliary, or participle not agreeing with essendo (essendo andato for a feminine subject who needs andata)

verb_form.imperativo.common_miss: using the present indicative tu -i for the -are imperative (parli! for parla!); or the wrong vowel on -ere/-ire (prenda! for prendi!)
negating the positive tu form (non parla!) instead of using non + infinitive (non parlare!)
regularising (va instead of va' / vai; fa instead of fa' / fai); using the indicative for essere/avere (sei! for sii!)
using the informal/indicative form for Lei (parla! or parli-as-indicative confusion); building the irregular from the infinitive instead of the subjunctive

verb_form.imperfect.common_miss: stress misplacement on 1pl/2pl
assuming the participle-irregularity carries over (preso giving presevo) instead of using the regular stem
carrying -isc- into the imperfect (capiscevo for capivo)
regular-ising to 'essevo' or carrying 'sono/sei' patterns over
using a regular stem (favo, faevo)
regular-ising to divo/dievo
regular-ising to bevvo
regular-ising to porre giving porevo
regular-ising to tradurrevo or traducovo
using passato prossimo for habituals (ho giocato instead of giocavo)
using passato prossimo for states (è stata stanca for the state, instead of era stanca)
swapping the tenses (ho dormito mentre bussava)
using PP (ho aspettato da venti minuti) instead of imperfect
using PP (ho avuto dieci anni, ha fatto freddo) for these states
missing the politeness reading and over-translating as 'I wanted'
defaulting to PP for English past-tense verbs regardless of aspect
using imperfect for the event-of-learning reading
interchanging the two and missing the completion implication
defaulting to imperfect for both because English 'could' is ambiguous
missing the insistence reading in the PP form
translating PP as 'have known' instead of 'met'
leaving the embedded clause in the present (ha detto che è stanco)
using future tense in the embedded clause (ha detto che andrà)
reading the imperfect as habitual when it's narrating a single event
translating literally and missing the counterfactual reading

verb_form.passato_prossimo.common_miss: using essere where avere is correct (sono mangiato la pasta)
sono andati (default masc plural with mixed or fem group)
le ho visti or le ho visto
Marco hanno parlato (wrong person on auxiliary)
ho mangiato già or già ho mangiato
Marco e Anna sono andate (last-noun agreement)

verb_form.passato_remoto.common_miss: treating an irregular strong-stem -ere verb (prendere) as if it took these regular endings (prendei for presi)
single i in 1sg (dormi), dropping the 3sg accent (dormi), or inserting -isc- (capiscii)
using the strong stem in the regular middle persons (presesti for prendesti); or regularising the strong persons (prendei for presi)
regularising (avei for ebbi, facei for feci); using the strong stem in middle persons (fecesti is wrong, it's facesti)
using a different past of the auxiliary (avevo parlato, the pluperfect) instead of the passato remoto (ebbi parlato)

verb_form.trapassato_prossimo.common_miss: using the present auxiliary (ho parlato) instead of the imperfect (avevo parlato); or a wrong imperfect-of-avere form
present auxiliary (sono andato) for the imperfect (ero andato); participle not agreeing (ero andato for a feminine subject who needs andata); wrong auxiliary

verb_form.present_indicative.common_miss: stressing the 3pl on the penult (parlAno) instead of the antepenult (pArlano)
using -ano for the 3pl (prendano) by analogy with -are, instead of -ono
inserting -isc- where it does not belong (dormisco for dormo)
verb_form.present_indicative.note: over-extending -isc- onto a plain -ire verb; covered by the broadened isc_over_extension (infix bleeding beyond its home).

verb_form.present_indicative.common_miss: carrying -isc- into the 1pl/2pl (capiscamo, capiscite) where it does not belong
omitting the h (ceri, pagiamo), which changes the consonant sound
writing studii (unstressed i should merge) or invi (stressed i should be kept)

verb_form.present_indicative.note: primary tag is the e/è accent; the sono 1sg/3pl homograph confusion has no dedicated seed (flag if worth one).

verb_form.present_indicative.common_miss: dropping the silent h (o, ai, a, anno), each of which is a different real word
regularising the 1sg (sapo for so, ando for vado) or dropping the accent on 'dà'
regularising the 2pl to dicete instead of dite
conjugating from the contracted infinitive (bero, bei) instead of the bev- stem
omitting the -g- (veno, teno) or carrying it into the wrong persons (vengiamo)
keeping the diphthong in 1pl/2pl (esciamo for usciamo) or losing it in the singular (uscio for esco)
regularising (poto for posso, volo for voglio) or dropping the accent on 'può'
wrong reflexive pronoun for the person (mi lavi), or omitting the pronoun (lavo for mi lavo)
verb_form.present_indicative.note: mi lavi = wrong reflexive person (the agreement seed); lavo-for-mi-lavo = omitting the required reflexive clitic (transfer).

verb_form.present_indicative.common_miss: progressive or future where the plain present is wanted
verb_form.present_indicative.note: usage bucket; discrimination-axis, same specifics as the tense_choice tree.
